suppress.notes: False positive (CPE mismatch). CVE-2026-42154 is a heap-allocation DoS in the Prometheus SERVER's remote-read endpoint (Go; fixed in Prometheus 3.5.3 / 3.11.3). Dependency-Check maps our Java client micrometer-registry-prometheus to cpe:/a:prometheus:prometheus, but this artifact only emits metrics in the Prometheus text format and ships no server code, so the CVE does not apply. Re-evaluate if the CPE mapping changes. Accepted by Jesus Obregon, 2026-06-13.

False positive (CPE/component mismatch). io.opentelemetry.semconv:opentelemetry-semconv is a generated set of semantic-convention CONSTANTS (attribute-name strings) with no executable code, so it cannot host a runtime vulnerability. Dependency-Check maps it to the generic cpe:/a:opentelemetry:opentelemetry product and so raises CVEs that belong to other OpenTelemetry components/languages: CVE-2026-39883 is a BSD `kenv` PATH-hijack EoP in the OpenTelemetry-Go SDK (affects 1.15.0-1.42.0, fixed 1.43.0). CVE-2026-39882 is flagged on the same constants artifact via the same generic CPE; its specifics were not verified locally (no NVD DB on hand), but the structural argument — a constants-only artifact has no exploitable code — holds regardless. Different component, so neither applies. Re-evaluate if this artifact ever ships executable code or the CPE mapping changes. Accepted by Jesus Obregon, 2026-06-13; CVE-2026-39882 added 2026-06-17.

False positive (CPE mismatch — wrong artifact). CVE-2025-7962 is an SMTP injection (CRLF) in Jakarta Mail / Eclipse Angus *Mail* (cpe:/a:eclipse:angus_mail, cpe:/a:eclipse:jakarta_mail). We depend on org.eclipse.angus:angus-activation — the Jakarta Activation framework (MIME/DataHandler plumbing), a sibling artifact with no mail-transport code; angus-mail and jakarta.mail are NOT in the dependency tree. Dependency-Check gates on the CVSS v3.1 score (7.5 >= failBuildOnCVSS=7) while the console prints the v4.0 score (6.0), which hides it. Re-evaluate if angus-mail / jakarta.mail is ever added. Accepted by Jesus Obregon, 2026-06-14.

False positive (CPE mismatch — wrong module). CVE-2026-34478/34479/34480/34481 are CRLF log injection in Log4j *Core*'s Rfc5424Layout (cpe:/a:apache:log4j, affecting 2.21.0-2.25.3). Dependency-Check tags every org.apache.logging.log4j artifact with that CPE, so it raises the same CVEs on BOTH the log4j-api facade AND the log4j-to-slf4j bridge (which routes Log4j2 API calls to SLF4J/Logback). Neither carries layouts/appenders, and log4j-core is NOT in the dependency tree, so the vulnerable Rfc5424Layout class is absent. Dependency-Check gates on the CVSS v3.1 score (7.5 >= failBuildOnCVSS=7) while the console prints the v4.0 score (6.9), which hides why the build fails. Re-evaluate if log4j-core is ever added. Accepted by Jesus Obregon, 2026-06-14; scope extended to log4j-to-slf4j on 2026-06-17 (chosen over a version bump, which would only mask a finding that does not apply).

Accepted risk (low exploitability) — NOT a false positive. DOMPurify 3.3.2 ships bundled inside swagger-ui (swagger-ui-bundle.js / swagger-ui-es-bundle.js), pulled transitively by springdoc-openapi 2.8.17 — already the latest release, so these client-side XSS sanitizer-bypass issues cannot be remediated by a version bump here. DOMPurify runs only in the browser that renders the Swagger UI docs page, whose content is the developer-controlled OpenAPI spec rather than attacker-controlled input, so exploitability for this API is low. Pinned to @3.3.2 on purpose: the suppression self-expires when springdoc/swagger-ui ships a patched DOMPurify, forcing a re-evaluation then (and consider disabling Swagger UI outside dev). Accepted by Jesus Obregon, 2026-06-17.
